column16: First I feel the name of the soundtrack would be borrowed time. This title would be based on its theme of time being only so limited and you only have so much until your time here is over. I feel like the first few songs would be about enjoying life. The first song could be nice and sweet representing a person being born into this world. Then the next few songs would be themed about your childhood and growing up. Some of these songs would be bright and some would be kind of sad representing mistakes. However, they would still be upbeat, representing a person cherishing their memories whether good or bad. Then somewhere in the middle of the soundtrack, you would be hit with a hard sombre song. This song would represent a person realizing that there is only so much time left until their time here is over. This song would be drastic representing despair and the songs to follow would be more and more dreadful as the person realizes there is nothing they can do as the hours tick by and they realize they have less and less time. However, the final song or two would then be bittersweet even uplifting. It would represent a person finally coming to terms with what is to come next. Coming to terms with the fact that their life here will eventually come to an end. However, they accept the fact and decide to live their life the best they can.

Like if I designed a soundtrack based on this painting, the first song would be sounds of nature like birds chirping, animals walking along the grass, leaves rustling, that kind of stuff. Then I would start with the bright music. In between every few songs, there would be an interlude of nature sounds again. Then the soundtrack would end with a bittersweet song followed by a final interlude of the sounds of nature getting quieter as the sun begins to set.
